Village Survey and house hold survey
The Unnat Bharat Abhiyan team of IIT Jammu started village survey in the month of
November 2019 along with the NSS volunteers of IIT Jammu. The baseline survey started
with the villages Tanda, Jahrri and Jagti of Jagti Panchayat and village Panjgrain of
Panjgrain panchayat of Jammu district. The village survey was followed by house hold
survey of Tanda and Jarri and Jagti. During the surveys the team found the major problems
faced by the villagers:
● Road connectivity
● Unaware of Govt. schemes like , old age pension, widow pension, kissan benefit
schemes of central and state governments

Response to Covid-19, awareness levels in the village and how the villages withstood challenges posed by COVID-19 Introduction In response to pandemic caused by SARS-COV-2 (i.e. COVID-19) our social responsibility as an academic institute of higher education has given us a pristine opportunity to work for the betterment of the society that we are a part of. IIT Jammu adopted 5 villages so far under Unnat Bharat Abhiyan, with almost 6500 population, coming under three Panchayats. We did a small study to understand the current situation of our adopted villages. We collected information to understand the awareness level, practice, challenges and how the villages withstood challenges posed by COVID-19. Methodology Information collected and verified through key-informants interview and field visits. Discussion Table1. Migrant labourers In the surrounding villages construction workers of IIT and IIM Jammu are staying and they couldn't go back to their native place. So far we have identified around 175 migrant families along with a large number of single migrant men (see table 3 for details). Many people are going through the crisis. IIT Jammu’s response In this regard the students of IIT Jammu under the NSS program and social welfare club have started some innovative initiatives that are as follows: 1 Distribution of food for Migrant workers The students are managing daily distribution of food to average 31 Migrant workers, from UP, Jharkhand and Bihar involved in construction works and stuck at IIT Jammu, as well as spreading awareness about COVID-19 among them. Leading by example, the students are also demonstrating the necessary precautions that need to be taken in order to limit the spread of the disease, as prescribed by the government and Medical Unit of IIT Jammu. 2 Online lecture series for Govt. We are working to help these villages as well, with the limited resources that we have without jeopardizing the safety of all involved. Heeng Cultivation in Ladakh UT, India: ------ a success story by Collaborative Efforts under UBA network by IIT Jammu Background: Heeng is a herbaceous plant of the umbelliferae family. It is a perennial plant whose oleo gum resin is extracted from its thick roots and rhizome. The plant stores most of its nutrients inside its deep fleshy roots. It is endemic to Iran and Afghanistan, which are also the main global suppliers of it. It is very popular in India and is used in cooking. It thrives in dry and cold desert conditions. The plant can withstand a maximum temperature between 35 and 40 degree, whereas during winters, it can survive in temperatures up to minus 4 degree. Regions with sandy soil, very little moisture and annual rainfall of not more than 200mm are considered conducive for heeng cultivation. During extreme weather, the plant can get dormant.Heeng is not cultivated in India. India imports about 1,200 tonnes of raw heeng worth Rs. 600 crore from Iran, Afghanistan and Uzbek

From May to early November 2024, the plants demonstrated vigorous growth.
Subsequently, in accordance with their established dormancy cycle, as confirmed
by IHBT Palampur scientists, they entered a period of winter quiescence,
characterized by leaf desiccation. The recent emergence of new sprouts signifies
the successful completion of this dormant phase. This resumption of growth is a
positive indicator of the plants' resilience and viability, and it is welcomed by all
stakeholders involved in the project.

## Unnat Bharat Abhiyan

Unnat Bharat Abhiyan is an important flagship program of Ministry of Education, Govt. of India.

## Vision of Outreach – IIT Jammu

The Outreach Program at IIT Jammu serves as a bridge between the Institute and society,
aiming to inspire, inform, and empower students, particularly from underserved,
remote, and border regions of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh.

Since 2024, IIT Jammu has hosted over **10,000 school students**
through routine requests, including students from government and far-flung schools. The program seeks to raise
awareness about science, technology, and higher education while fostering curiosity and ambition among students
with limited exposure to such opportunities.

## Key Components of Outreach Department

Exposure to advanced laboratories, research activities,
and r

### Vigyan Jyoti Scheme

Encouragement of meritorious girl students to pursue STEM education and promote gender equity.

## Objectives

• To inspire students through exposure to advanced laboratories and real-world applications of science and
engineering.

• To enhance learning through hands-on demonstrations and guided lab visits.

• To promote interactive faculty–student engagement for academic guidance and inquiry.

• To provide mentorship for exploring STEM career pathways.

• To encourage peer learning through interaction with IIT Jammu students.

• To ensure inclusive outreach to students from remote and government schools.

• To support the Vigyan Jyoti Scheme for promoting STEM education among meritorious girl students.
